title: chenopodium ambrosioides subsp. obovatum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides subsp. obovatum is a subspecies of the annual herb Chenopodium ambrosioides, also known as Mexican tea or epazote. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, antihelmintic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Chenopodium ambrosioides subsp. obovatum include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and flatulence, and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments.
